title: Macro Matters: BI’s Adelberg on Housing, MBS and Prepay Risk
summary: Housing affordability, mortgage-market modernization and changing refinance behavior are reshaping the outlook for mortgage-backed securities. Erica Adelberg, Bloomberg Intelligence MBS strategist, joins Ira Jersey, BI chief US interest-rate strategist, on this edition of the FICC Focus podcast, Macro Matters. title: Oil Traders Crowd Into Cheap TACO Hedge as Iran War Amps Up
summary: An unusual options trade is becoming a favored hedge against the risks of sudden U-turns in US President Donald Trump’s stance on Iran, a dynamic that has repeatedly whipsawed markets in recent months.

title: China Beef Curbs Start Rippling Across Brazil Meat Industry
summary: Brazil is starting to feel the impact of China’s beef import quota, with lower shipments to the country’s largest overseas market reverberating across the entire supply chain.

title: Chipmaker Rout Hits Stocks on AI Spending Worries: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with a resurgence in geopolitical risks also weighing on sentiment.

title: Korea ETF’s (EWY) Record Inflow Fuels SK Hynix Proxy Play
summary: A record inflow hit the largest US-listed South Korea exchange-traded fund, as investors seeking exposure to SK Hynix Inc. piled into the ETF as a proxy at a time when the chipmaker’s new American depositary receipts trade at a substantial premium to its local shares.

title: Chevron Plans to Sign Iraq Oil Accord, Seeks Hormuz Bypass
summary: Chevron Corp. expects to sign accords with Iraq on Friday that will advance its negotiations about investing in large oil fields and a pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil dipped, though is still higher for the week, as traders balanced profit-taking with concerns about disruptions to Middle East flows.

title: Emerging Currencies Dip as Dollar Gains Over Iran Worries
summary: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as renewed tensions in the Middle East sparked a flight to haven assets, pushing the dollar higher.

title: Fed’s Logan Favors Modestly Higher Rates to Lower Inflation
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan called for higher interest rates, saying inflation does not appear to be heading sustainably back to the central bank’s 2% target.

title: Apollo Bets on Mexico With $20 Billion Target for Private Credit
summary: Apollo Global Management Inc. plans to put as much as $20 billion to work financing projects in Mexico as the giant asset manager seeks new outlets for its private credit business.

title: Balbec Secures $930 Million in Commitments for Asset-Based Credit Fund
summary: Alternative asset manager Balbec Capital LP has obtained commitments of more than $930 million for its latest flagship fund that will invest in a variety of asset-based credit, according to a filing on Thursday.

title: Paschi Casts Doubts On Intesa Bid, Urges Studying BPM Plan
summary: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA expressed broad concerns over a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo SpA while highlighting perceived benefits in a rough rival pitch presented by Banco BPM SpA.

title: Podcast: Tokenization, Digital Twins and the Future of ETFs
summary: On this episode of Trillions, we explore how this government transformation will affect stocks, bonds and exchange-traded funds.

title: Turning Oil to Fuel Has Never Been More Profitable as Refining Margins Surge
summary: Profit margins for US refiners making gasoline and diesel from crude oil are shattering records, as fuel supply disruptions driven by war lift prices for refined products.

title: Citi Traders Pitch Shorting French Shipping Giant CMA CGM Bonds
summary: Citigroup’s credit traders and analysts are recommending their hedge fund clients short bonds issued by French shipping giant CMA CGM Group, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: European Stocks Post Small Gains After a Day of Volatile Trading
summary: European stocks recouped the day’s declines to end modestly higher, with investors monitoring renewed geopolitical risks. ABB Ltd. fell 5.9% after a deal to buy British industrial components maker Rotork Plc.

title: Ladbrokes Owner Entain to Cut 2% of Staff as UK Taxes Hit
summary: Entain Plc will cut 500 jobs, or about 2% of its global workforce, as the gambling industry grapples with higher UK taxes, tighter regulation and growing competition from prediction markets.

title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
summary: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a decline in assets over the past three years.

title: India’s Maruti to Fight Order to Replace Car Over Ethanol Damage
summary: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. said it will challenge a consumer court order asking the nation’s largest carmaker to replace a vehicle over alleged damage from ethanol-blended fuel, a case that assumes significance as the debate over the nation’s biofuel push intensifies.

title: Wheat Wavers After Touching Two-Year High Amid Black Sea Strikes
summary: Wheat fluctuated after touching a two-year high as traders weighed ongoing Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea and weak US export data.

title: Aston Martin Creditors Tap Jefferies as Debt Worries Grow
summary: A group of creditors to Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc have picked Jefferies Financial Group Inc. as financial adviser as concern over the debt of the distressed British automaker grows, according to people familiar with the matter.
